text: UAE closed its entire national airspace with all flights at Dubai International suspended. This is the world's busiest international airport handling 90+ million passengers annually.
verificationNote: UAE aviation authority and flight tracking data confirmed

text: Senior IRGC Quds Force commander Brig. Gen. Hossein al-Askari killed in drone strike on Baghdad's Green Zone. Was coordinating militia operations across Iraq — a major escalation of targeted killings.
verificationNote: Multiple Iraqi sources and IRGC media confirm

text: Oil dropped sharply — Brent fell 4.3% to $100.21 and WTI dropped 6.1% to $93.50 after Treasury Secretary Bessent said the US will allow Iranian tankers through the Strait of Hormuz. WSJ reports the US is building a multinational naval escort coalition.

Iran has sent at least 11.7 million barrels of crude through the Strait of Hormuz to China since the war began. Shadow fleet vessels continue operating despite the blockade that has halted all Western commercial shipping.

IDF announced it killed Basij commander Gholamreza Soleimani, his deputy, and IRGC Aerospace Force chief in overnight strikes. Described as the biggest leadership decapitation night of the war so far.
verificationNote: Based on IDF announcement; awaiting Iran confirmation

text: UAE defense ministry reported 304 ballistic missiles, 15 cruise missiles, and 1,627 UAVs intercepted since the conflict began — a staggering number of Iranian projectiles aimed at Gulf states.
US and Israel have discussed sending special forces into Iran to secure its stockpile of highly enriched uranium at a later stage of the war, according to Axios sources familiar with the discussions.
verificationNote: Axios citing sources familiar with discussions

text: French President Macron declared after G7 video call that 'No one can tell what US President Trump wants from this ongoing war with Iran.' Israeli officials provided conflicting war objectives to G7 leaders.
verificationNote: Macron statement after G7 call confirmed by Elysee

text: Day 18 daily report: IDF moved 36th Division deeper into southern Lebanon while cluster munitions hit central Israel. 37 Hezbollah attack waves identified on March 16. IDF expanding buffer zone toward Tyre.

Iran-linked cyber proxies have become highly active since the war began. MOIS-linked 'Stryker' cyberattack demonstrates Iran retains high-end offensive cyber capability despite kinetic degradation of military targets.

Updated Iran civilian casualty count to 1,351 civilians killed including 207 children, plus 1,126 military personnel and 622 unclassified deaths. Most detailed independent casualty tracking available.

Joe Kent resignation is huge — first senior Trump official to quit over the war. His claim that Iran posed 'no imminent threat' directly contradicts the Article II legal justification. Expect Congressional subpoena.

Kuwait-flagged tanker Gas Al Ahmadiah struck near Fujairah — 23rd vessel damaged since blockade began. Iraq's oil minister in direct talks with Iran to negotiate passage for Iraqi tankers through the strait.
verificationNote: Satellite tracking and Iraqi government statement

text: USS Gerald R. Ford heading to Crete for repairs after 30-hour onboard fire. ~200 sailors treated for smoke inhalation. Carrier temporarily out of theater — significant gap in US naval power projection in the Eastern Mediterranean.
verificationNote: USNI News confirmed via Navy sources

text: Russia expanding intelligence sharing with Iran per WSJ — satellite imagery of US force locations plus improved Shahed drone components. Iran's targeting accuracy against US assets has noticeably improved since Ukraine-era drone tech transfer.
verificationNote: Based on WSJ report citing European intelligence officials
